title: Atlantic Basin Crude Exports Experience Slowdown
section_path: Atlantic Basin Crude Exports Experience Slowdown
section_path_text: Atlantic Basin Crude Exports Experience Slowdown
summary:
Main
Crude supplies from Atlantic Basin experience declines amidst slowdown in East of Suez markets but find some relief from demand within the basin. By Rohit Rathod
Atlantic Basin emerged as the marginal supplier in 2023 for global markets hit with OPEC+ supply cuts and managed to keep the markets well supplied keeping crude prices in check. Come 2024, the momentum seems to have slowed a bit, not due to supply side issues, but to slowdown in demand from East of Suez markets, mainly China.

children.summary:

Atlantic Basin emerged as the marginal supplier in 2023 for global markets hit with OPEC+ supply cuts and managed to keep the markets well supplied keeping crude prices in check.

children.title: Atlantic Basin crude going to East of Suez Markets slows
section_path: Atlantic Basin crude going to East of Suez Markets slows
children.section_path_text: Atlantic Basin crude going to East of Suez Markets slows
children.summary: January 2024 saw a big drop in Atlantic Basin crude exports to East of Suez markets with a decline of 1.7mbd vs December 2023. Majority of this decline was driven by the Americas as they sent barrels to Europe instead.

children.title: Americas crude heads to Europe instead of Asia
section_path: Americas crude heads to Europe instead of Asia
children.section_path_text: Americas crude heads to Europe instead of Asia
children.summary: Focusing on the Americas, which have driven the growth in Atlantic Basin supplies, we have seen a steady increase in their volumes heading to Europe. In January 2024, Europe surpassed Asia as the preferred destination for Americas crude mainly driven by European appetite for US light sweet crude.
